Hey, welcome back to Straight Line Marketing, Module 5. Now, this module is essential to your success as a Straight Line Marketer. Here we're going to learn the secrets to getting massive amounts of new traffic. One of the keys to the success of Straight Line Marketing is that by applying its principles, not only will we be able to convert potential customers at an astronomically high rate, but you'll also maximize their value to the highest possible level. However, in this module, we're going to teach you how to actually find these customers in the first place. You see, attracting huge amounts of traffic is the difference between a good campaign and a mind-blowing one. You can learn a step-by-step system for doing this that will be able to implement your business starting today. Take out your workbook, write your notes, and I'll see you on the other side. Alright guys, Module 5. Now you all know how to build a kick-ass funnel. It's time to point some traffic to it. See the hard part's over. You guys now have the skills to build a sales funnel that converts it a really high rate, in fact much higher than your competitors. And you've also learned how to extract the most value from each converted prospect. With these skills, buying traffic is a piece of cake. Now, you know that you can spend much more acquiring costs than your competitors can, which of course means you can then grow at a faster rate and start consuming a greater portion of the market. But before we can start sending traffic to our funnel, we need to know how effectively to buy traffic. We need to know where to buy the traffic from, and how to effectively target the traffic using sound, straight-line marketing principles. Remember, you need to be able to achieve the three tens, but we can effectively close a sale. And that means we need to target prospects that are most likely to already love our product, have some trust in us, and connect with us, and then trust and connect with the company. Now if you've targeted your traffic poorly, then trying to achieve those three tens becomes almost impossible. Remember, not everyone's closeable, and we shouldn't attempt to close everybody. See, the straight-line marketing system is designed to empower us if you do it to close everyone who's closeable. So we find the closeable prospect through effective and efficient traffic targeting. Make sense? Good. So as always, here's Dave to walk you through the actual traffic buying methodology. All right, guys. Dave here again for module five of the Jordan Belfast Straight-Line Marketing System, the Traffic Secrets module. Now this module is really cool because since you guys understand sales funnels now, you understand why we have sales funnels, how we built the sales funnels, the value of the great sales funnel, namely to extract the most amount of value from every single customer, which enables us to spend more than our competitors to acquire that same customer. Now the only missing element is just pointing traffic to that sales funnel. So we've got it all built, all the web pages are built, we know the sequence, we're going to run all of our customers through. Now all we've got to do is send traffic to that actual funnel, and that's what this module's all about. And you know, often when I speak about traffic, it's often met with a little bit of fear and concern. It sounds scary to some people for some reason, and I'm really not sure why, because traffic is a commodity. Like Jordan said, it's been so heavily commoditized now the days of the old traffic hack, especially with organic traffic, you know, the black hat SEO strategies are all but dead, they're just about over now. It's nothing I ever want you guys to consider or think about. I don't want you to play the SEO game. Okay, here is a thankless game, something I got very good at probably about five or 10 years ago, and was reaping some some pretty great rewards with the skill set that I had developed. But those days are long gone. You know, there are some people still trying to play that SEO game, and frankly, I feel sorry for them because it's not the way to build the business anymore. And you can't you're not afforded, and I'm talking about SEO players here, they're not afforded the opportunity or the luxury of having a reliable and predictable mathematical relationship between their ad spend and their return, or that they can't control their scale in other words. So for instance, you guys would have an awesome sales funnel. And once you start pointing some traffic to it, and you look at your conversion rates, you may say that, okay, now I know, based on how far the average person makes it down my funnel, I earn say $100 for every single customer. Now, I'm just using arbitrary for you guys. So you earn 100 bucks a customer $100 for every customer. So you know now, you can spend, say, depending on your overheads, how much, how much expenses you have outside of traffic, you can say hypothetically, you can spend $80 to acquire that customer, right? You can spend $80 to acquire that customer. Okay, so you have $20 or say, $15 miscellaneous expenses, whatever its expenses might be, and you have a $5 profit margin, right? So we're just speaking hypothetically, these are arbitrary. So you know, you can spend $85 to acquire a customer that's going to be worth $100, and you have $15 in expenses and $5 in profit, you know that. So all you need to do now, and provided your sales funnel is converting at a rate where you can spend that amount of money, all you need to do is keep on spending. You can keep on buying traffic and grow your business to whatever, whatever the vision or goals you have for that particular project. So nothing stopping you from scaling, you know, the only thing that's limiting you is the market and your own ambition. Now, the same can't be said for organic traffic, right? You could be ready to go, you could have the capacity, you know, business could be going great, you could have a respectable margin or net margin with respect, of course, to the actual product sales. So the amount of cost you to produce a solar product versus the amount of money you get in return for having sold it, but you've got nothing to do, you can't scale. So you're stuck, you're bottlenecked, right? There's nowhere to go because you can't scale organic traffic. traffic, the hard part is done, and I know that's a real mind shift for a lot of you guys, so a lot of new online marketers, even pro online marketers right now are really caught up on traffic strategies, traffic hacks, and you know, if I target this particular word in this particular market, if I target based on URL as opposed to keywords, if I watch the advantages of CPM over CPC, and All right, so here we go, our goal when we're buying traffic is to profit at or at least break even at the point of sale. Okay, so you guys are all familiar with that sales funnel now, right? Remember that's why we had our SLO, our SLO ideally was our break even point, okay? So when we're purchasing traffic, when we're buying traffic, and we're sending it to our sales funnel, remember they're coming in on an opt-in page, we haven't made any money yet, right? All they've done is given us their email address. Now after they've submitted their email address, they're immediately made an SLO or self-liquidating offer, and the objective of that SLO is to break even, right? We want to whatever we had just spent in acquiring that customer to get it back in our SLO or the profit from a SLO, we want that to break even. So hypothetically, you spent $7, you've given a particular traffic network $7 to acquire a particular customer, the customer's gone through the sales funnel, they've opted in, they've had a look at your SLO, they've converted at the SLO level, and they've just given you $7, now you've broken even, right? So you haven't made anything from that client, but you haven't lost anything from that client, you're not in the black, you're not in the red. So here we are. So where do we make our money? We said we're going to make our money through with our core product, right? We're going to make another upsell with our core product, then we have a series of upsells and downsells, and they remove onto a thank you page, of course, they're optional, but the core product is not optional, it's mandatory. But what about if you don't profit at the SLO stage? What about if you make it all the way to the core product stage and then you break even? Was that a good campaign or a bad campaign? Have a think about it. You haven't made any money. So the prospect has gone through the opt-in, they've opted in, they've gone through your SLO, they've purchased it, they've gone to the core product, they've purchased it, but you had spent so much money acquiring that customer that you have just broken even at the core product stage, not at the self-liquidating office stage, which is our ideal scenario. Is it worth it or not? You haven't made any money, you haven't lost any money, is it worth it? Now I really wish you guys would give that some deep thought, and I wish I could hear you guys so I could ask you what your thoughts are. I'll talk you what the answer is. The answer is yes, it was still worth it. Because think about it. Remember we spoke about the value of capturing that email address. Remember how we said that now that we have that email address, we can promote other offers to this particular prospect. We know what they want, we know what they're interested in now, we can just send them emails whenever we like, promoting a particular product, you're going to be a kick-ass email marketer once you guys get onto the email market, email marketing module that's coming up. And what else can we do with email? We can use it as part of our funnel follower, remember our funnel follow-up system where somebody dropped off at a particular spot, we can actually make other offers to them or excuse me, we can resell ourselves or our company or our product, of course, try to achieve those three 10s and move them onto the next step of the sales funnel. Again, we're going to cover all that stuff in detail once we get onto our looping module, but we did speak about it at least very briefly. So we know there's real value in an email address. You can absolutely take an email address and cash it in. I really want to impress upon you the value of an email address of that data because that's real money. And I don't want you to think of it as a name on an order responder list, that we're going to be talking about order responders, but I don't want you to think of it as a collection of email names on an Excel spreadsheet because that's not what it is, it's real money. Every time you collect an email, you're making money. So if you break even at the core product, at the core offer core product level, right, we've just made that core product offer and they purchased and we still haven't made a profit, but we have broken even then that campaign was successful. It was a success because we have their email address, we can send them emails to try to push them to through the upsells or the downsells or we can make different offers to them and you will make money from that email, from that email address. It's happened like it is about, I'm about a certain of that reality as I am of anything an email address is absolutely mandatory and it is real money. If you're able to collect data at break even, so you're able to collect an email address at break even haven't made any money when the prospect has traveled all the way down to core offer of our sales funnel, the campaign is still successful. I still want you guys to scale that campaign. So if you're testing it small, if you're able to break even at that point of sale, scale it. I just want you to blow that up because as far as I'm concerned, you're building a list and you're doing it for free and that's real money there. So there's our golden rule. Our goal when we're buying traffic is to profit or at least break even at the point of sale. Of course, if we're able to break even at the self liquidating office stage and then profit at the core office stage and then profit even further at our upsells through our upsells and downsell sequences, then definitely scale just keep on scaling scale scale. Give that campaign as much money as you can because you're just putting a dollar at the top of the funnel and you're taking two, three dollars out and you just want to be doing that all day long. So there's our goal guys, profit or at least break even at the point of sale. Here we go again. You guys must have this rule tattooed to your arm, you know, or at least have it hanging up here in your office for the next couple of weeks. And I know I've said it a million times and I will say it a million more because it's that important and I want to impress it upon you guys that this is the golden rule. Whoever is able and willing to spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer wins. And that's a huge mental shift and I know it is. I mean, I experienced it many years ago. My idea or my philosophy was attract customers as cheaply as possible, right? Spend as little as possible as you can in acquiring the customers and have a greater margin. So your expenses are down. It's not how it works in the real world. It simply isn't. You want to be able to spend the most amount of money on every customer. You can spend $10 to get that customer into your funnel and your and your competitors, excuse me, can only spend five. Who's going to have more customers? Who's going to take up more market share? Who's going to be growing at a faster rate? You know, you guys are going to be, of course, you know, you're able to spend more money. You're able to scale faster and harder. You can take more customers and clients away from from your competitors and you can capture as much market share as you want and scale as fast and as large as you want. So I really want to impress upon you guys again, the importance of our golden business rule. This is our, this is an SLM fundamental. If you haven't got on board with this concept yet, accept it as a reality, it will, it's mandatory and it will change your business forever. Remember, whoever is able to able and willing to spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer will win. All right, let's talk about traffic platforms. So we understand the idea of sales funnels. We understand that all we need to do is send traffic to a particular sales funnel. And as long as it even breaks even, we're winning. If it's actually profiting at point of sale, we're really winning. Okay, that's scale time. Go, go, go. And all systems go. You want to scale that as fast and as hard as you can as quickly and aggressively as you can. And we understand the golden wall that if we're able to spend more money in acquiring a customer, then we can grow and scale at whatever rate we want. We can capture as much market share as we want as long as we're continuing to, as long as we're prepared to continue to spend that money in acquiring the customers, nothing can or will slow us down. We can go as fast and as aggressively and as large as we like. So we understand all those fundamentals. Now the only thing left is actually going to the platforms or the traffic channels and buying the traffic and sending it into our sales funnel. Let's take a look at some of the platforms. We have YouTube, we have LinkedIn, we have Google, Twitter, rented email lists, Facebook, banner ads, PPV traffic, CPA traffic, content discovery platforms. Now your head's probably spinning, right? That's a lot of different traffic platforms. What do I use? What's the value of each one? Are any of them right for me? Are all of them right for me? Which one should I start with? How do I buy traffic on YouTube? How do I buy traffic on Twitter? Guys, it couldn't be easier. Okay. And I'm going to go through most of these traffic platforms. I'm going to teach you the basics and how to buy traffic. And I'm also going to give you guys a cheat sheet that will help guide you through this traffic overwhelm. You're going to find all the platforms and it's going to list a platform and list when you should be buying traffic from that particular platform as opposed to another. And we'll go through that once as we move on through the course. But I just wanted this particular slide to introduce to you some of the different traffic platforms we use, some of the ones we like, and some of the ones that I think you should try depending, of course, on your business and offering. And I say that with a caveat because the last three, PPV, CPA, and content discovery platforms, I'm very much on the fence with them, especially with content discovery platforms only because our ROI has never been, never been really great. I mean, we have broken even sometimes broken even excuse me sometimes PPV traffic, I'm not a fan of it. And I'll explain why once we get onto PPV. But at least these are the traffic platforms I would want to introduce to you guys, even if we're not using all of them. And I'll explain to you when I think you should be using each of them and the advantages of using one platform over another. All right, let's go on to this particular business principle guys, which is Dan Kennedy's marketing triangle. Those of you who've been around the marketing world will know Mr. Dan Kennedy, he's been around for as long as marketing has been around probably. And he's come up with this really interesting display. And the reason I'm sharing it is because it's actually especially pertinent to what we're talking about now, when we're talking about traffic. This is the display explained. Okay, so you have at the top, we can see the market. Now the market is whoever it is, we're selling our product to. And then we have on the bottom right hand corner, message, message, I want you guys to think of the message as nothing more than your copy and your sales letters, then all the words you actually put out to the market to sell whatever it is you're selling. And on the bottom left hand corner, we have media. Now for our purposes, I want you guys to think of media as traffic, your traffic platform. All right, so now we've got market who we're selling to, message what we're actually going to be telling whoever it is we're selling our products to or making the offer to. And then we have media or all the channel or traffic source we're using to actually speak to that particular market. All right, so the marketing triangle display tells us this, we need to have all three working perfectly well together in order to convert a prospect. So we need to have the right market, the right message, excuse me, to the right market, and it needs to be presented or delivered to them through the right media platform. Okay, so we're comfortable with the message now, right? Guys, we understand copyrighting, we understand the value of crafting an awesome message. But if we send that great message to a total mismatch with respect to the market, right? So if we send a, let's say, let's think of a really obvious example, if we sell a women's weight loss supplement, all right, so that's our product, we're selling a women's weight loss supplement, and we've crafted the world's best piece of copy, just the greatest, highest converting sales letter you could possibly imagine. And we presented that sales message to a group of men, what have we done wrong there? We have really high converting mark message, but we targeted the wrong market, right? We haven't sold it to the right person, we have the wrong market. Now, equally important is the actual media. Now, the media is nothing, media is nothing more, excuse me, than the medium that we decide to choose or decide to make use of. In this case, what traffic platform we're going to be using. So for instance, if I am, and we'll think of another really obvious example, if I am selling a, let's say a perfect product for a perfect market, let's say I'm selling an arthritis product, okay, so it's an arthritis supplement that has been specifically designed for over 80s, for seniors who are over the age of 80, okay, and I have the perfect message, great piece of sales copy, I have the perfect market over 80s, but I'm advertising on Facebook, do you think I'm going to do it? I can tell you there aren't too many over 80s on Facebook right now, even worse, sales advertising on Twitter. Twitter has a significantly younger user base than Facebook. Do you think I'm going to accomplish much? I have a great piece of high converting sales copy, I have a perfect target market, but I'm using the wrong media, right? I'm using the wrong channel. So that's the value of the marketing triangle, we need to have the perfect market, we need to have the perfect message, excuse me, to the perfect market, we need to reach them through the perfect media, and right now we're talking about media or traffic, but we can't of course, target the traffic unless we know who it is we're speaking to, so we need to know more about the market. So let's have a chat about market, we're perfect with messaging, we understand the value of sales copy, we understand concepts like the action threshold, adding up or stacking up the positives, knocking off the negatives, we understand the three tens, love our product, love us, love our company, we understand that sales copy is little more than script writing in the written form, where we can actually put it up online, we understand copy, we're already ahead of 95% of everybody online with respect to our messaging. But one thing that we're not, we haven't really nailed into or we haven't really explored deep enough is of course market and media. Now we can't start with media, we can't start with a platform unless we know who it is we're targeting, okay, because we need to pick the right media platform based on our particular market. So let's talk about how we can find our market and markets to them effectively, your customer avatar, gathering market intelligence. Now your customer avatar, now this is interesting, I like to, and I was taught this many years ago, and it's proven to be a very helpful aid, what I like to do is create a customer avatar. So here we are, we're trying to determine who exactly our market is, who our ideal customer is, who is it that's likely to be of most value to us, who is it, excuse me, that we're most likely to be able to extract the most value from with respect to our potential customers or our prospects. Now to shortcut that process, I was taught the concept of the customer or consumer avatar, and I haven't looked back ever since being introduced to that idea. Now what an avatar is guys, you want to find your ideal customer and try to create a fictional character, you can go as far as actually giving this character or figure a name, I want you guys to know how old he or she is, obviously whether it's male or female, their interests, their education level, their income levels, I want you to find out as much information as possible about your avatar. Now once you actually really nail it down, you know who your ideal customer is, then buying traffic is a cinch, because we know exactly how to target our traffic appropriately. So not only do we know how to target, we also know where we can buy our traffic from, right, because that's the media portion of our marketing triangle. We're going to go through that as soon as we're done here, but right now I want you guys to create your own customer avatar, I want you guys to think of who your ideal customer is, and collect as much information on him or her as possible, and give this figure a name, and even when you're writing your copy guys, or writing your persuasive sales letters, I want you to think and think, excuse me, as though you're writing to that one single person, that particular customer avatar. Now you guys have a customer avatar template in your resource section, this is the template I use every time I'm running a campaign, when I want to know exactly who it is I'm talking to. So that template will allow you to identify your market like with real great precision, so precision, excuse me, so you can tailor an awesome message that targets that market really precisely, and allows you of course to convert, allows your sales letter to convert at a much higher rate than if you were taking a shotgun approach. So this is the sniper versus shotgun, it's really targeted, it will really help. So download and print your template, fill it in as best you can, and don't stop at one. So you guys might be saying, okay, well, I don't think I can decide between male and female, for instance, you know, I'm evenly split, my customers are evenly split between men and female, that's fine, create one avatar that is male focus and create your ideal male with respect to age, interests, all of it, the whole nine yard to go through the entire template and fill everything out, and do the same for a woman and fill it out in its entirety, leave no gaps guys, because all the information you guys use to fill out that avatar template is not only going to be used for our copywriting, it's also going to be used for our targeting. Okay, so we're going to know exactly who to target, and it's also going to be used when trying to determine our traffic source, right, which traffic platform is best for you, a lot of it has to do with who your customer avatar is. So if you were to ask me, David, what's your favorite traffic source right now, what are you seeing the most wins it wins with, you'll get a blank look from me because it depends on a whole lot. And one of the key factors is who exactly am I marketing to. We went through earlier that silly example of the 80 year old seniors who are being presented with an offer on, excuse me, some kind of arthritis medication, I think it was, it did illustrate the point really well, though, how on earth could we possibly use Twitter, even if Twitter is a super high converting platform, and everyone's experience or a lot of people, excuse me, are experiencing a lot of great wins on Twitter with awesome conversion rates, and they're buying traffic cheaply, how could on earth could we possibly enjoy any kind of success advertising on that platform, if our customer avatar, if our ideal customer isn't even on, isn't on the platform at all, they never have a Twitter account, they've never logged on to Twitter, so I don't care how great the platform or channel is, it's not right for us. So I want you guys to really nail this customer avatar exercise, really go through it in as much detail as you can. I want you guys to craft an awesome avatar that's as detailed as possible, if it helps, I know I do what it might sound silly, but I like to give my avatar a name, you know, I could have a John, Mary, Steve, or whatever, it adds some meat or some bones to my avatar, so it makes him or her real for me, so I like to actually give my avatar's names, don't skip this step, and you might think it's a little airy, and you know your customer, you don't need to fill out the, I promise you, you're going to be able to buy traffic more effectively, you're going to be able to select channels more effectively, you're going to be able to write copy more effectively, and it will produce awesome, awesome wins. So download your template, it adds a cheat sheet for avatar writing, you guys don't need to give it any thought, all you need to do is answer the questions, fill in those gaps in the avatar template, and you'll have your own avatar. I would like to see you guys have about five avatars and rank them, so you would have your first avatar, give it a name, and that's your ideal, ideal customer, that's the gold standard, then you will have a second avatar, that is the second best, and the third best, and so forth, and the reason I like you guys to rank them is because the greatest avatar or your favorite avatar, which is of course the avatar that's most likely to spend the most amount of money with you, right, and happy customers spend more money, that ideal customer, you would actually be able to spend a little bit more money on targeting that particular person, so for running a Facebook campaign, for example, and where we're targeting men, obviously you're going to target a lot more precisely than just by gender, but just for the sake of the example, if you're targeting men, for instance, you could be, you may be prepared to target them for, you know, X dollars, and women for X plus five dollars, and a woman in your female avatar, excuse me, is way ahead of the male avatar, so you're prepared to pay that difference, pay a little bit more money, you can attract more females into your sales funnel, or greater quality female into your sales funnel, because you know that you're able to attract more value from that particular avatar as opposed to the other, so put your avatars together, rank them, identify the avatar that has the most amount of value to you and to your business, versus those that have lesser and lesser value, please do at least five, but if you're not going to do that, I want mandatory three awesome avatars, three very detailed avatars, and it's going to make the process of buying traffic and writing, copying and selecting traffic channels or platforms a real piece of cake. Alrighty, so the avatar lets us know who they are, we're talking about our market here, so avatar lets us know exactly who our market is, we know our message, we covered message pretty in pretty great detail in our copywriting module, so now the only element missing in our marketing triangle is the media or the medium of traffic source, we need to reach them, how are we going to reach them, what are we going to use to get that message to that market, alright so that's all about traffic, now here we go, good golden wool for you guys, there's no one size fits all traffic source, your ideal traffic channel depends on your goals and your product, okay and I'll add another one, it also depends on your avatar or your customer, alright so that's why I told you guys, ask me what my favorite traffic channel is and you're not going to get an answer, I can't give you an answer because there are so many factors that are at play here and the idea is have an awesome customer avatar, know exactly who you're talking about and that's going to make it easy to pick a platform, okay, also find tell me more about your product, what kind of product do you have, I mean we can't use for instance a trending platform now is Pinterest, you know, I'm starting to see a little bit of traffic come from Pinterest, I don't love the platform myself, but I'm starting to see a little bit of traffic from there, what about if you're selling a digital product, I mean what use is Pinterest to you right now if you're selling a digital product or you know a product that isn't so image focused that it doesn't have so much beautiful imagery and Pinterest won't be of much value to you guys, will it, what about if you're a local Brooks and Mortar business, you're a local dental practice for instance, for those of the dentists that are listening, you try to purchase a solo ad for example, on a mass email marketing list, right, so you like an Archimax list or there's a list of say millions of people from all around the world will receive a particular email about your dental practice, it's a very little value to you right, because you maybe you may be based in New York for example, you're sending out an email blast to people all around the world, from Europe to Australia and so forth, it's not the right channel for you, it doesn't have the kind of targeting options that are suitable for a local Brooks and Mortar store, it's more of a mass market channel so it's that would be more suitable for a product for example like a weight loss supplement that can be shipped around the world or a weight loss ebook you know some kind of a digital product, maybe that'd be a better or more suitable fit, so there's no one size fits or traffic source, your ideal traffic channel depends on your goals, your product and of course your customer avatar, I can't stress that enough guys, all right so we really all remember the marketing triangle guys, we've got the market, the message, the media, so we've done message, we've done market and now we're on media, all right so what traffic channel is right for your business, okay you want to know what traffic channel is best for your business, ask and answer this question, where is your avatar likely spending his or her timeline, think about it, we need to reach these guys online, we're talking about online marketing right now, online traffic targeting, so they need to be online at a particular place doing a particular activity and wherever that place is we can target them, okay so if they're spending a lot of time on Facebook which a lot of people are, that's why Facebook's a pretty great platform to start with depending on your business of course but it's a great platform to start with and the reason we often start with Facebook is because almost all markets are there and a lot of people are there if you think of that, if you're targeting young folks from 19 to 25, they're on Facebook certainly, from 30 to 45, they're on Facebook, from 50 to 60 they're on Facebook and you can do real laser targeting on Facebook, I mean you can target based on interests, I mean you can target down to people who like tea and biscuits and I mean that literally, if you want to, if you have an offer that's marketed to people who you know also happen to like tea and biscuits, you can actually target that, I mean the targeting options are mind blowing, so I often recommend starting with Facebook only because if I was going to have to pick one particular channel, it's quite often, Facebook quite often suitable, again you guys have a marketing channel cheat sheet and take a look at that and go through it, see whether or not Facebook may or may not be suitable for you guys but it's quite often a great start, whether you're you know a mass market producer of a particular product or a mass market marketer, may not need to necessarily produce it, or you're a bricks and mortar local business, quite often Facebook still going to be the answer for you guys, so that's why I typically recommend Facebook, now in addition to that the actual user interface or excuse me the advertisement interface, the back end to actually get your ads up has become really easy, it's not difficult to use at all, now the targeting like I said is laser like, you can target just about anything or anybody who likes just about anything as well, you can target people who own homes, you can target people who own particular cars, but you may want to target people who don't own homes, not from a home loan product, whatever it is you want to do, you can virtually target anything or anybody on Facebook, targeting capabilities are mind blowing, I love it for that reason, now the other reason is that we said earlier that we need to find out where exactly our ideal customer or our avatar is actually spending their time online before we can pick a channel, now here's the great thing about Facebook, it now records 1.55 billion monthly active users, these are active users, they log on regularly and these are monthly figures, we're talking 1.55 billion, now with those kinds of numbers, it's a safe bet that whoever it is you're marketing to is going to be using this platform, they're going to have a Facebook account and they're going to be logging on to it pretty regularly, now that is of course a bit, still a bit, we don't know, we don't know unless we know your product and you know we have to run a few more tests and ask ourselves a few more questions before we can say that with certainty, there's a good chance that your customers are going to be on Facebook, which is why I recommend you get started on there, depending of course on your business and it allows for awesome scale, so scale is a big thing for me, we've spoken about it throughout this module, that's why I love paid traffic generally, so I can test small, run a couple hundred dollar campaigns, find the winner, you know I sent it to my sales funnel, I realized that I either profited at the point of sale in my sales funnel or better still I, excuse me I broke even, or better still I actually profited at the point of sale in my sales funnel, in which case at scale time I would scale massively on Facebook because it has that kind of reach, it can reach though huge numbers and basically everything that it would scale, it can scale, I have very rarely seen the situation where I wanted more scale and Facebook didn't have the room, I actually have had a few situations where that was the case but I mean there are few and far between, quite often as large as you want to go, Facebook has the capacity to accommodate and that's one of the reasons I love it, for its scale capability as well. Alright now, so you guys might be convinced a lot about Facebook, you may not, but the next question is, the next question of course is whether or not it's right for you, now I'd argue that it will be right for you, I mean of course like I said I need to know more about your business but it's likely that it's going to be a good fit and for that reason even if it's not the best fit, I still would like to see you guys test your sales funnel, its conversion rate, test the amount of value you're able to extract from the average customer who actually passes through your sales funnel, test it on Facebook, it's just a great platform and its targeting ability, really excites me and it has excited me and it's only gotten better over the last few years, so I do encourage you guys to get your feet wet on the Facebook platform, it's a really easy platform to use, it's not challenging at all, I mean what I recommend you guys do is actually go through your cheat sheet sooner rather than later, take a look at all the particular traffic channels, download them, print them, mark them and just have a tick of two or three right now that you really want to test and try out, I would recommend Facebook to be one of them but it doesn't have to be, you may really decide to stray away from Facebook and you can look at your traffic cheat sheet and say okay well it looks to me that ad swaps are a really good idea or excuse me email ads or rented solos, rented email lists, that might be a really good idea for me, I have a product that's a good fit for an email, I would rather that over Facebook, I mean it's totally up to you but I do want you guys to pick three traffic channels, two to three traffic channels that where you're ready to master, I want you guys to master those particular channels, if you have to start at one and you're saying to me I don't want overwhelm David, you warned us against it, you promised no overwhelm right at the beginning of the course so you're going to mitigate that overwhelm, I only want to cover the one particular traffic model, that's fine, the only reason I like comparing traffic channels excuse me is that you really do get a feel of how different channels produce different results and how your actual media or the actual platform you use is such a significant factor when determining your overall customer value or the amount of value you're able to extract from your customer, that's the reason I like people to go through more than one channel when they're actually learning traffic, learning how to buy traffic effectively and efficiently, I really do encourage you to go through more than one at the same time which is counter cultural and a lot of people argue against it, master one system and then move on to the next, but you know it's fine if you really want to do that and you really only want to test the one platform right now and then move on to the next one later, that's fine, I'm going to introduce you guys to a lot of platforms and you're going to know exactly where to go when you want to learn the actual interface and the actual ad buying interface and that's fine but just be mindful of my advice and that is starting off with two or three channels at the same time, give you guys a feel of how different channels operate, how different channels mean different lifetime values of customers and of course the scale capability on different channels is wildly different, you know you may find one channel where your campaign is kicking ass and you really want to press the campaign and amplify your ad spend and grow it really quickly and then all of a sudden your bottleneck is somewhere and you can't grow anymore and you'll speak to your network, you know you send them emails, you'll speak to your rep and say I want to spend more money, I want to grow but the actual network doesn't have the kind of reach at least not with respect to your target, you know if you have to target a particular person, a particular avatar, it doesn't have the kind of reach that you would really want or you really need to scale so it bottlenecks you or stops you at a particular point and there's nothing more frustrating than having an awesome campaign and not being able to scale and you're going to be able to notice those things if you test a few different channels simultaneously which is why I recommended but again if you really want to limit or mitigate overwhelm it's fine to get one particular source or one channel master that then move on to the next channel and by the time you move on to the next channel you'll experience all of those or all the benefits or the learning experiences associated with comparing one channel against another and comparing and contrasting the results. Okay so let's look at our first channel it's Google now Google's the you know 800 pound gorilla out there online and it has enormous amount of ventures especially in its reach you know you probably second to none I don't think anybody can reach the kind of bias that Google can or the kind of prospects that Google can. What's especially interesting about Google is it's search platform right so Google's broken up into two particular platforms or two most the most popular platforms of two particular platforms and that is all three because we're talking Google AdWords so actually YouTube advertising is actually part of Google AdWords now for those of you who don't know since Google acquired YouTube but let's talk about the two most significant parts or components of Google AdWords you have the search network and you have the display network the search network is great for people who are in market you know for looking for prospects or targeting prospects who are actively searching for whatever it is you're selling so for instance someone can write into the search box dentist close to Philadelphia or Philadelphia dentist if you happen to be a dentist in Philadelphia that's a great place to put your ad right because you want somebody who is actually looking for a dentist so we're talking about a kind of marketing approach that's called in-market targets people who are looking for your service offering as opposed to pushing a message in front of people who although they may be ideal customers they're not actually actively looking for whatever it is you offer now the alternative to search marketing in Google is the Google display network and the Google display network is something we'll talk about a little bit more once we get on to banners but the Google display network is just a series of images and text ads that are all across Google partnered sites so for example I may have a website and Google will and I will rent some inventory or some space inventory some space on my website for Google to serve advertisements of banner ads or serve text ads on the actual page and then Google will take that inventory sell it to an advertiser and they will take a certain amount of money for having done that and then they'll pass on some money to me as the actual owner of the website so that's the way the Google display network works and that's the way the search network works now we can go into the platform and have a play what I really recommend though is you guys pick up the Google AdWords fourth edition by Perry Marshall uh microbes and Brian Todd it is an awesome easy to read book it's a resource I strongly recommend and I have recommended it to all of my students in the past flick through it have a play decide whether or not you want to advertise on the search network or on the display network and actually get your ads up there test one ad against another and the process is so simple it is not hard at all the challenging part with respect to marketing is actually getting the great fun of working right and the challenging part with respect to actually buying traffic you're just targeting the ads properly and making sure you have a good message and we are all expert message writers now we can write great ads and great sales copy because we've all gone through our copywriting module we understand the importance of writing persuasively now in terms of actually physically going into the network and buying the ads and then putting them up online that's so mechanical and it's so simple it's easy all you need to do is know where to go all right so the first place we've had a look at and quick discussion about is google let's have a look at the next big 800 pound gorilla okay so here we are we're with facebook now facebook is awesome I strongly recommend it for all the reasons we've said earlier so we don't need to spend too much time on the platform on this particular ad I love this targeting ability I love the reach I love it's scale potential it's an absolute awesome platform and something I strongly recommend you guys give a go now I'll give you guys a little secret weapon that I use when running facebook ads pretty frequently and that is a software solution called choir that's q w a y a q w a y a now it's not inexpensive I think it's about 149 USD a month for this particular software but it's really brilliant in that what it does is it allows you to create as many facebook ads as you like okay then it allows you to split test them against each other and then you can actually time the ads so you might say I only want this ad to run this particular time between this time of that time then turn off and turn on this time in this time it allows you to rotate the ads so you can actually rotate different elements of ads to see what's working best it's just an awesome solution and best of all you can do it all within its interface so you don't need to log on to facebook you don't need to log on to the editor you don't need to touch any of that you know so if any of that frightens you you don't want to you know create a facebook advertising account and put your ads up and you just want to do without all of that head eight and you can go into choir and manage the whole process from there and it could not be simpler now if you're going to take advantage of choir what I recommend you guys do is go into the training section and take a look at the videos or read the training read the support section so you can understand exactly how to set it up how to set up your ads there's also a great blog there that talks about some of the best kinds of ads that convert really well to a particular market and be mindful of that when you're when you're testing any platform that if you have any questions at all there's always a support section there are always ad reps who want to help you remember it's their business they only get paid if you guys are actually spending money on their platform so they want you guys to spend as much money on the platform as you can and they want you to keep on coming back and the only way you guys are going to come back of course is if your ads are performing and you're at least breaking even at the point of sale so they want you to do well they want to help you so read all the resource sections read the support sections and you'll be pleasantly surprised at how easy it is to get your ads up online whether it's on google or facebook and how how unassumingly fun the process can be you know I really enjoy it at the moment strangely enough a lot of people think it's a little too mechanical and it's mechanical but I like targeting my ads and I love looking at the reach and scaling and the growing and looking at the campaign performance of seeing whether or not I can or should scale you'll enjoy you'll learn to enjoy the process too and it's pretty fun and exciting especially when you finally hit a home run after building so many sales funnels and buying so much traffic you actually hit a home run and maybe you're gonna you guys are gonna love it because there's such an empowering feeling to be able to just you know give facebook your credit card and just increase your budget and say you know go and make me some money you know so I love it and I'm sure you guys are going to as well now I've actually got a recommendation as well a resource recommendation and that's perry marshall and Keith Kranz's facebook advertising I think they're in the second edition the third edition might be coming out soon but it's inexpensive enough for you guys just pick up the edition that's out when you guys watch this video get this module again the actual ins and outs of facebook are explained in pretty great detail but in a fun easy to understand and learn way and again remember guys we're talking mechanics we're talking transactional stuff when we buy traffic we are not talking about any of the real marketing work you know the real persuasive marketing work which is the real tough part which is the stuff we spoke about when we were building our funnel and when we were talking about our copywriting and we're talking about the straight line fundamentals right for the actual facebook advertising stuff and all the traffic stuff what we're talking about here is mechanical stuff and you'll notice the actual process of buying traffic is not going to be real challenging for you guys because you guys actually have your fundamental internet marketing terminology down pat so it's not going to be so stressful or difficult to buy traffic because you know what cpc is right guys it costs per click you know how much you know what that's all about you know you can purchase traffic on a cpc basis or a often depends on the network of course or a cpm basis and what's cpm that's cost per thousand impressions so the thousand times people have seen your ads every thousand times you've seen somebody's seen your advertisement you'll have to pay an agreed rate as opposed to paying only once they've clicked your ads right you guys know about click-through ratios and the importance of actually getting people to click the ad once they've seen it right so yeah if any of those terms have slipped your mind please go through the fundamental module again or go through your terminology cheat sheet to even save yourself more time and just have a look at those look at the key terms and make sure you can define them all if you're familiar with the terms and you actually have a play around the interface of whatever network you decide to go with or whatever ad platform or channel you decide to experiment with first you'll find that it's actually an easy and fun experience and you will be glad you did all right the next one we're going to talk about is email traffic this is an interesting traffic source now we know the importance of email marketing and you're going to you're going to have that impressed upon you even further once we get onto our email marketing module but you don't need to necessarily get traffic from an advertising platform or a platform where people are actually um or a platform where people are actually visiting so you don't need to excuse me target your prospect on a website that he or she is on at the point of time where they see your ad another way for you guys to get traffic is by actually purchasing traffic through or renting somebody else's email list now we know the value of collecting our own email list right so we can actually send our all of our subscribers different offers or send them an encouragement to move further down our sales funnel there's also an an opportunity for us to send an email to people who are not on our list we know the value of emails right we know emails work we know emails can get people to come back into our sales funnel can get people to move into a brand new sales funnel of ours we can send emails to people who haven't opted into our list by actually sending an email to somebody or to a prospect who's opted in to somebody else's list okay so what that's called is a solo ad now let's just go on to the next slide now a solo ad is the process of sending an email to somebody else's email list maybe to a portion of their email list to test it first and then if it works scale it and send it to the entire list depending on the size of the list of course and paying that list donor a certain amount of money for actually running that solo so it doesn't even need to be a big network for instance you could go online right now go on to google say you're selling a dog training product product that trains puppies you can go on to google write something as simple as dog training blog or dog training forum or dog training article anything along those lines okay and have a look at all the results and see the web pages that have an opt-in box remember what an opt-in box does guys an opt-in box converts the actual visitor into an email subscriber they have to give us their email to take advantage of it of a bribe or of a lead magnet or of an offer a free offer so go online take a look at all the blogs that are collecting emails that is all the blogs they actually have an opt-in box now if they have an opt-in box it means they're collecting emails which means they have an email list and you yourself are selling a dog training product so what you would then do is contact the website owner go into the contacts find their details and then send them an email and say i would like to negotiate a solo buy with you i just want to test a small segment of your list if things go well then i'm happy to email to your entire list how much will it cost for a solver and anybody who sells solos they're going to know what that means so don't need to explain the process of solo lists to them they all understand it but that's what a solo list is going to somebody else's list giving them a certain amount of money will the privilege to be able to being able to send an email to their entire list and in that email of course there's going to be a link a link so going to be some compelling copy you guys know how important the copy is right by now it's everything right you guys i'm sure you're starting to see and appreciate that it's going to have some compelling copy and that copy is going to solve the click or encourage them to make that click to click the link and what's that link going to be you've got it it's the link to our sales funnel it's the link to our opt-in page right as the objective there is to get as many people on that other person's list to get on to our list and get into our sales funnel and we're going to pay that list owner for that privilege that's the simple way to find solo solo list owners just go on to google and and type your whatever service it is you offer and then write the word blog after it try to find as many blog owners that are actually have an opt-in box with me to their collecting a list make them an offer to or actually ask them what it would cost for you to actually mail out a solo mail out that solo with some great copy encourage encouraging the the visitor or subscriber to click your link once they've clicked your link the sales funnel will do all the heavy lifting and convert that prospect into ideally a high value customer now you don't need to go about it that way okay you don't need to go about it that manual way of actually going and trying to find ideal ideal list owners by searching their blogs or searching forums or whatever you can do it a much faster way and that's by actually just going to networks now i've listed a few networks there for b to c offers that is of course business to consumer offers and at the bottom i've listed a few networks for b to b offers or people who are in the business to business space now what a network is it functions the exact same way as a regular list owner would function these guys have massive lists that like we're when you talk about archimax for example we're talking millions and millions of people are on those lists and they collect those lists um through really above board regular double opt-in subscribed visitors who took advantage of uh or who want to excuse me to take advantage of our weekly newsletter or a monthly newsletter that archimax puts out so archimax will come in take you know archimax have a great website um in a lot of different spaces one space may be political based website catering to you know some political faction whatever and uh they encourage anyone who's landed on that website to subscribe to a monthly newsletter where they're going to get all this great content that's relevant to whatever their interests might be and in exchange for that weekly newsletter you would have to submit your email address and they're in that business that's what they do full-time collect email addresses okay so they built an enormous list of email addresses and all you have to do is go to archimax and tell them oh look i'd like to mail you know this is my particular offer do you have a relevant list ask them or take a look at their categories and say do you have a a list that will be relevant in this particular offer for this particular offer excuse me or for this particular product they're really great honest people over there um they won't try to screw you if they don't think they have a great list for you or they don't think the list is a good fit that they'll let you know but even bearing that in mind they still with that much that notwithstanding i still want you guys uh once you find a good list a good segment of archimax list test it small test it as small as you can uh and then if it works you know so you've tested it only one little small segment of the list let them know you only want to uh send a soul about to a small segment and if it converts at the level you would like it to convert and people are going through the sales funnel and uh you're making a lot of money you're breaking even a property from the sales funnel and what you would do is you would then scale get back in touch with them say it all went well arena test was perfect i want to email you know more of the list now i want to widen or broaden my reach now with archimax you have a couple of options you can actually do a solar which means you send it directly to the people on the list and everybody who's subscribed on the list have double opted in and double opted in means they've opted in to take advantage of whatever it is that archimax was offering them and then archimax will send them another email with the confirmation link and say are you sure you want to sign up you know to to activate your subscription you would need to click this particular link to confirm um that particular action and they all do that which means it's a cleaner higher quality more responsive list since it's a double opt-in they have strict double opt-in policy there at archimax which is good so you have an option to actually email that list list directly and send it straight to them alternatively you can um take advantage of a sponsorship type add and since they're actually sending out newsletters you can actually have your advertisement um in a lot of different formats in text format or banner format or by way of a newsletter sponsorship um so so you have a few different formats you know the solar is going to be the most expensive but it's also almost always going to convert highest but you will do well to actually speak to the to good folks at archimax and they're they're actually my favorite b2c email network but you have a few options there you can also speak to a lot of brokers okay you can see if you broke is there there are brokers function the same way as are at any broker would so what they would do is you would explain to this particular broker what it is you actually sell or offer if you go through these options you'll find some brokers in that list what it is you actually sell or offer you know what is your product you know what is your service whatever it is you're trying to sell online and what they would do is then they'll go all to all the networks and all the solo lists owners that they happen to have um have met or or networked with in the past and they'll try to marry your offer with the best list and in exchange they'll take a certain amount of commission so that that that can be you know a great win you know I haven't had too much success with brokers but that's only because I really haven't played that market as aggressively as maybe I should or would you know I will I might actually give it a go and spend a little bit more time with brokers typically I'm going directly to the networks but brokers could be a great option for you guys especially if you don't want to get your hands dirty actually negotiating with the network owners and then you want somebody else to do it all for you and just pay that person a commission you know using a email broker mightn't be the worst idea so you have your b2c office there all you have to is google that name and then you'll get the actual URL and then you have your b2b office as well launch launchbit nextmark and info usa both launchbit nextmark and info usa I think they're they're both interchangeable so you could do excuse me you could use them for both b2c and b2b office most of my work in email marketing almost the success I've been doing in email marketing has been the b2c space not the b2b space I typically find business owners less responsive to email marketing adverts than regular consumers because typically you have a work email and people who are at work and they're receiving emails too quickly quick to delete or ignore emails that aren't especially relevant to them at that particular point in time not to say it won't work for b2b or can't work for b2b but I have noticed a bit more success in the b2c space than in the b2b all right let's move on to our next channel media buys now media buys is just a have an interesting name for banner advertisements so a banner advertisements is an advertisement you see online that's an image right as the display you can have those scrapers banners you know all those little rectangular banners or so forth and a banner is you know a great traffic source for people who know how to buy them only because they're cheap they're dirt cheap and you can quite often buy them on a cpm basis or on a on a pay per thousand views basis so you're talking thousand people have seen that particular ad and you guys are smart right you guys know what a frequency cap is you know that you're not going to let the same person see the ad 100 tons in one day right so you know about that 24 hour period you guys have gone through your email fundamentals module so you're familiar with that as long as you have your frequency cap in place and you have your targeting pretty solid you can get some really cheap traffic from this what I want to warn you guys against is actually trying to waste your time in design software whether it's in a photoshop or or the little banner design software platforms that are popping up everywhere now trying to create your perfect banner you know don't waste your time with that I mean you guys are marketers here you're not designers your market is here you're not developers remember marketing is where all the money is you know actually getting somebody to design your banner shouldn't cost you more than five or ten dollars go on fiverr.com have a look at the different banner designers have a look at their portfolio email message one of them give them your instructions what your product is maybe a picture of your product or just a compelling headline and a picture of your logo whatever it is and tell them you know get me to create me a kick ass banner you know and how much is it going to cost you it's going to cost you a whole ten bucks a whole ten dollars you know at very most so I wouldn't waste too much time actually creating banners if you want a whole banner pack so all the different types of sizes of banners so you can test one size against another on a particular network and then that's fine you know buy a whole pack from a particular designer and buy it for you know 50 to 100 dollars and you have like seven eight different banners and test the actual effectiveness of one size and one color design versus another and so forth better go to fiverr.com to get your banners designed also recommend upward that's upward dot com so you can get very cheaply designed banners there now once you actually have your banner so you've gone to either fiverr they've given you a great banner um so you found a um a contractor who's uh who you like their portfolio and you've explained your product and they passed on there and they've created and given you an awesome set of banners or you know advertisements little colorful advertisements and different sizes and shapes and you're ready now you've so you've got your banners on hand you're ready to actually put them out into the internet so people can click on them and once they click on them where are they gonna go you've got it you're gonna go onto our opt-in page of our sales funnel so once you have those banners where do you go so here's where you go guys you got the google display network remember how we spoke about google at the beginning in the beginning it was one of our first traffic channel sources we said you can go into google display network and um and take advantage of either the search network or the gdn or the google display network now that's what google adwords had so the third and most important section in google right now is youtube and google adwords is youtube but for now we're talking about the search network and the display network now this is where you're gonna put your banners you're gonna put them into the display network all right so you're just going to go into google go into the display network fill in some basic information upload your images and make sure your targeting is on point and then you're off you're off to the races you're on your bike it's all that's required and then you just sit back and you watch and you determine whether or not the results are the results warrant you to start spending more because you're only spending a small amount at first to test the campaign so you determine whether or not it warrants the scale or whether or not you're going to pull it off completely because i'm not happy with those leads at all i'm not breaking even at all so i'm going to pull it off completely and you then make the decision whether or not you should change the banner so actually change the image that's going out there or the words on the image for example or change the targeting maybe you're targeting the wrong person you might be doing manage placements for example the manage placement it's just say for instance um you know where your visitors are going to be so you may have an offer that's just perfect for fox.com foxnews.com it's perfect for foxnews.com you know that all of your customers are foxnews.com readers and all foxnews.com readers are a perfect ideal customer for you they're a perfect fit so you might tell google put this particular ad on foxnews.com put this banner on foxnews.com and you tell them where it's going to go that's what we call manage placement alternatively you can let facebook do the work right and facebook can go ahead and tell you where it thinks the banner is going to enjoy the most amount of success excuse me so there's two several different targeting options on the gdn but it just gives you an idea you may have a really great campaign with a really great advert that's likely to convert really well and you have really great sales funnel that all the traffic's being said to but because you targeted incorrectly or you didn't use the best targeting approach the campaign didn't work so just be mindful of that so we're going to try to break even or profit from all of our campaigns if we fail in either what we're going to do is try to test the copy if we choose to we may fail absolutely um terribly and just pull it off the platform completely and say i don't want to use this platform anymore i don't want to use the gdn i'm not happy with the gdn i don't want to use google display network i want to go and try facebook you know i want to you know go and try some email buys and i want to rent an email listen that and buy a few solos but if you decide to stay on the network there's troubleshoot time and the way you're going to manage that process is of course by changing the copy changing the actual design of the ad changing the targeting okay they're going to be our three most significant um factors determining the success or failure of a particular campaign provided that the channel itself isn't the issue all right so we're not limited to google you can accomplish the exact same thing on a few different networks now google is notoriously strict with its advertising policy so if you're advertising or trying to sell anything that's even remotely um questionable i wouldn't use the gdm your account will get shut down pretty quickly if you have an exit pop on your landing um on your opt-in page so for instance if people have just landed on your page they've clicked the image the banner then they've landed on your landing page they try to get out and they get a pop-up an exit pop you're going to make google very angry if there's autoplay if they land on a video on your opt-in page and it plays automatically all the controls are hidden you're going to get into a whole lot of trouble with um with google so just be mindful of that they have very strict advertising policy so sometimes um some advertisers choose to ignore gdm entirely depending on what product they sell and how large their market is and so forth and they may use different different networks to purchase banners there are a few other a few options you've got buy sell ads iSocket you've got xo click we're media group ad onion you've got a few different options there none have the real powerful reach the gdm does but all of them have um far less stringent advertising policies so you know um if you're running a real clean above board campaign now gdm should do it 80 pages online google ad sense pages so they actually generate revenue through google ad sense which means they're actually selling inventory space to google google's putting the banners up there and then paying the advertiser and paying the web page owner so your reach is pretty phenomenal your scale is unsurpassed second to none so provided you actually satisfy the advertising policies there really isn't too much of a need to explore other networks when trying to buy banner ads or purchase media but again if google has shut down your account um you know it's not all failures it's not all over excuse me you do have other options there are the networks you can use and if you're concerned that you're offer or your product or your actual funnel isn't exactly perfectly google compliant then you do have other options as well if you would rather purchase traffic elsewhere as opposed to changing or making your funnel and especially your landing page more compliant the landing page of course is your opting page your squeeze page or lean page it's just the first page they land at when they've clicked either the the link in the third in the case of these search results ad or the actual image in the case of a banner ad or the text in the case of another ad on the gdm because the gdm doesn't only allow for banners you can also just put text so just regular words on the gdm as well okay and and google will serve it across all the ad sense websites which is of course around 80 percent of the internet or another interesting ad platform and ad channel is safe swaps now you guys understand the concept of email so you understand the value of actually collecting emails and you also understand the value of sending out emails to collected lists you guys understand that we can actually pay another list owner a certain amount of money whether it's a regular single small list owner or a major network with all these with millions of email addresses we can pay them a certain amount of money for the rights or privilege to send their list a particular email and of course in that email there's going to be a link and that link's going to direct the traffic right to our lead page which is the first page in our awesome slm sales funnel now you don't always have to pay for that and not an alternative to actually accessing lists other people's lists is too bad you can tell one list owner for example i'll email your list and then you can email my list in exchange so i may have you have 15 000 subscribers on my list and you may have 15 000 subscribers on your list there's a good chance that people on your list like the products that are on that i'm selling there's a good chance that people on my list like the products you're selling let's swap so there's this a whole online community that's based around ad swap so everybody's got a list and they'll set up a calendar and i'll email you all this your email my list and and gets a little crazy but it works you know depending on your product especially if you have a mass market product ad swaps might be a good idea um you don't have the kind of targeting capability you certainly don't have the kind of scale opportunity that you do with the other platforms that we've mentioned which is why i just want to introduce you guys to the concept of ad swaps not necessarily promoted as the most ideal traffic channel but it's absolutely an option there especially for those you already have a list and don't want to spend any more money driving traffic for whatever reason you know there might be some financial constraints preventing you from doing so so know that you can still attract some good quality traffic by simply bartering email messages or as if you want to know more about ad swaps or familiarize yourself with that particular channel i've put the URL there on the screen take a look um hopefully you can have some success with this particular platform but just remember your targeting capabilities are heavily limited and of course your scale potential is nothing like some of the other channels we've already discussed all right cpa you guys are familiar with cpa because we've spoken about it in the past but we've spoken about it from the perspective of course of an affiliate right so affiliate marketers would find affiliate offers and they would then purchase traffic and send that traffic to that particular offer and then if they convert the sale the vendor will then pay the affiliate a certain commission and then we went one step further and says and and said that they don't even need to always actually make a sale they can simply just accomplish a particular action or goal so the vendor may have a product and the product could be um something as simple as uh so the uh the their sales funnel excuse me could be something as simple as getting an opt in and that opt in leads to you know a free 30 trial offer or of a particular supplement and and then once that offer once that 30 day period um expires or matures the prospect can then um the prospect is then encouraged to renew their membership and become a paying monthly customer now we said that you didn't actually need to make a sale all the time like an affiliate or the vendor may simply require an affiliate to accomplish a simple action and action could be something as simple as submitting an email address so a vendor may create an offer put it up on on a cpa network like offer vault.com which is one of the largest cpa networks and then the vendor will simply encourage affiliates to point traffic to that particular lead page and get the email address and as a result of getting that email address without the prospect making any purchase whatsoever all you've accomplished here as an affiliate is is they're encouraging the visitors submit their email address and as a product of that these the vendor will then pay the affiliate or having successfully sent and converted traffic to accomplish that particular action in this case the action of course is submitting an email address and the vendor will then take event then the vendor then will claim that email address and put them further through their own sales funnel now here's the thing if you function as a vendor in this case not an affiliate you may say i know exactly how much each subscriber is worth to me so you guys know exactly how much each person who opts in to your sales funnel is worth to you you can do that math very simply right you just look at the amount of money you've made over a certain period of time you look at the amount of subscribers you gained in that particular time as well and you divide the amount of money by the amount of subscribers and then you know exactly how much each subscriber is worth to you now as a result of that what you're able to effectively do is actually put up an offer on a cpa network like offer vault and encourage affiliates to send traffic to your sales funnel with the objective of only getting an email address so you can tell your affiliates it doesn't matter whether or not the prospect buys anything all you need to do is get them to put their email address into this particular opt-in form and as a result i'll pay you this certain amount of money and you know exactly how much you're prepared to pay then because you've done that math you know the value of every subscriber so this is a good safe way to build to really grow your list again it depends on the products you're offering you know so so go through your cheat sheet alternatively just go on to offer vault.com and take a look at the different products that different vendors have put up there and see whether or not your product can fit in any of those categories and if it can and you've already tested your sales funnel through other channels this cannot i repeat this cannot be your first channel you can't test this one and just try to master cpa cpa traffic without having obviously uh paid your dues with other traffic channels okay so go into the other channels you know exactly how much each subscriber is worth to you uh you you know how your funnel converts and you're able and you're prepared to actually scale because everything's working and looking really good then you can go to a cpa network pay the affiliates whatever it is you think is fair or whatever it is you think is a commercially sound figure to accent traffic into your sales funnel and collect those email addresses and hopefully you'll be able to convert a whole heap of them your cpa campaign will be a runaway success all right the next traffic channel i want to speak to you guys about is there's another controversial one and it's ppv now what ppv is or pay per view traffic is i'm sure you guys are familiar with that you've been exposed to it i'm not no one
